title: Major UK Porridge Brand Recalls Products After Mouse Found in Production Facility
description: Moma Foods recalls porridge products months after discovering mouse contamination at third-party manufacturing facility.
lede: A popular UK breakfast brand has recalled multiple porridge products after discovering mouse contamination at a production facility. Moma Foods announced the recall months after the initial contamination event occurred last autumn.

sections.heading: Contamination Discovery Details
paragraphs: Moma Foods confirmed that a third-party manufacturing facility experienced what the company described as a "mouse contamination event" during autumn 2025. The discovery prompted an immediate investigation into affected product batches.

The contamination was identified through routine facility inspections, though the company has not disclosed the specific timeline between the incident and the public announcement. Food safety protocols require immediate action when such contamination is detected.
Consumer safety concerns have prompted the company to take a precautionary approach, recalling products that may have been affected by the contamination incident.

sections.heading: Product Recall Scope
paragraphs: The recall affects multiple porridge varieties manufactured at the compromised facility. Moma Foods has advised customers to check batch numbers and expiration dates on their products to determine if they are affected.

Retailers across the UK have been notified to remove affected products from shelves immediately. The company is working with distribution partners to ensure comprehensive product withdrawal from the market.
Customers who purchased affected products are advised to dispose of them immediately and contact the company for full refunds, regardless of whether the packaging has been opened.

sections.heading: Health Risk Assessment
paragraphs: Mouse contamination in food products poses several health risks, including potential exposure to harmful bacteria and parasites. Rodent droppings and urine can transmit diseases such as salmonella and E. coli.

Health authorities emphasize that consuming contaminated food products could lead to gastrointestinal illness, particularly in vulnerable populations including children, elderly individuals, and those with compromised immune systems.
The Food Standards Agency has been notified of the incident and is monitoring the situation to ensure appropriate public health measures are implemented.

sections.heading: Company Response
paragraphs: Moma Foods has issued a formal apology to customers and emphasized their commitment to food safety standards. The company stated that they are conducting a thorough review of their third-party manufacturing partnerships.

Enhanced inspection protocols have been implemented at all production facilities to prevent similar incidents. The company is also reviewing its supplier vetting processes to ensure compliance with food safety regulations.
A dedicated customer service hotline has been established to handle consumer inquiries and refund requests related to the recall. The company promises full cooperation with regulatory investigations.

sections.heading: Industry Impact
paragraphs: The incident highlights ongoing challenges in food manufacturing oversight, particularly when companies rely on third-party production facilities. Industry experts stress the importance of rigorous supplier monitoring.

Similar contamination incidents have affected the food industry in recent years, leading to stricter regulatory requirements for facility inspections and pest control measures in manufacturing environments.
Consumer confidence in breakfast product brands may be affected, though swift action and transparency in handling recalls can help maintain trust in the broader market.

sections.heading: Consumer Guidance
paragraphs: Customers are advised to check their porridge products immediately and dispose of any items with matching batch codes. Refund processes have been simplified to minimize inconvenience for affected consumers.

Food safety experts recommend always checking product recall notifications and maintaining awareness of contamination incidents in commonly consumed products. Proper food storage and inspection remain essential.

The incident serves as a reminder for consumers to remain vigilant about food safety and to report any unusual odors, appearances, or foreign objects found in packaged food products to manufacturers and authorities.

faqs.q: What products are affected by the Moma Foods recall?
faqs.a: Multiple porridge varieties manufactured at the contaminated third-party facility are affected. Check batch numbers and expiration dates on product packaging to determine if your products are included in the recall.

faqs.q: What health risks does mouse contamination pose?
faqs.a: Mouse contamination can transmit harmful bacteria like salmonella and E. coli, potentially causing gastrointestinal illness, especially in children, elderly individuals, and immunocompromised people.

faqs.q: How do I get a refund for recalled porridge products?
faqs.a: Contact Moma Foods through their dedicated customer service hotline for full refunds. Refunds are available regardless of whether the packaging has been opened.

faqs.q: When did the mouse contamination occur?
faqs.a: The contamination event occurred during autumn 2025 at a third-party manufacturing facility, though the public announcement came months later as part of safety protocols.

faqs.q: Is it safe to eat porridge from other brands?
faqs.a: This recall is specific to Moma Foods products manufactured at the affected facility. Other brands and facilities are not implicated in this contamination incident.
